events.label: reasoning
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Unsealed weatherhead penetration allows moisture and debris to enter the service entrance conductors and metering equipment.",
    "Potential for corrosion, short circuits, and ground faults due to water ingress.",
    "Non-compliance with NEC 230.54 which requires weatherheads to be arranged to prevent entrance of moisture."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately seal the weatherhead penetration with an approved sealing method (e.g., duct seal, sealing fitting) to prevent moisture ingress.",
    "Inspect service entrance conductors and metering equipment for any existing moisture damage or corrosion.",
    "Verify that the weatherhead is properly installed and oriented to shed water away from the entrance."
  ],
  "rationale": "An unsealed weatherhead penetration is a serious safety hazard because it can allow water to enter the service equipment, leading to corrosion, insulation breakdown, and potential electrical faults. This condition violates NEC 230.54 and must be corrected before the installation can be considered safe.",
  "references": ["NEC 230.54"]
}
